name: Bean Sidhe
description: The Bean Sidhe, also known as the banshee in Ireland, is a female spirit in Scottish folklore, often seen as an omen of death. She is considered a fairy woman, though not one of the Seelie or Unseelie courts, but rather a solitary figure tied to specific families or clans. Her wailing lament is said to foretell the imminent death of a family member, particularly those with Scottish or Irish heritage. Though her appearance can vary, she is often depicted as an old woman with long, flowing hair, sometimes dressed in rags or a burial shroud. Her cry is described as piercing and otherworldly, a sound that chills the bones and fills the heart with dread. While her role is primarily as a harbinger of death, some tales suggest she can also offer guidance or warnings to those she is bound to, though such instances are rare. The Bean Sidhe is deeply connected to the ancestral lands and the lineage of the families she watches over, making her a powerful and respected figure in Scottish lore. She is not inherently malevolent, but her presence is a somber reminder of mortality and the cyclical nature of life and death. Her existence is a testament to the enduring power of folklore and the enduring connection between families and their past.
subtitle: Wailing harbinger of death.
appearance: Often depicted as an old woman with long, flowing hair (sometimes white or grey), dressed in rags or a burial shroud. She may also appear as a beautiful young woman, but her eyes always hold a deep sadness. Sometimes she is seen washing burial shrouds in a stream.
habitat: Ancient Scottish lands, near the homes of the families she is bound to, often near streams, lochs, or burial grounds.
behavior: The Bean Sidhe is primarily known for her wailing, which is a lament for the dying or soon-to-be-dead. She is often solitary and elusive, appearing only to those who are about to lose a loved one. While her presence is feared, she is not actively malevolent, but rather a messenger of fate.

keyMyths.title: The Washer Woman
keyMyths.description: The Bean Sidhe is sometimes seen washing the burial shrouds of those about to die. The color of the shroud indicates the manner of death.

sources.author: W.Y. Evans Wentz
